name: teacher_cert_ai_assessment_cross_border_bias_claim
premise: Credential board chair Dr. Catherine Blackwell must decide whether to accept AI-scored teaching demos for immigrant teachers seeking reciprocity after a study shows accent bias in prosody scoring. Provost David Okafor argues manual panels cannot clear the backlog before fall semester. Keeping AI speeds hiring for underserved STEM classrooms; removing it may violate equal protection if bias persists without mitigation.

agents.name: Dr. Catherine Blackwell
agents.identity: I am the credential board chair accountable for interstate reciprocity agreements, classroom quality gates, and anti-discrimination monitoring in high-stakes assessments.
agents.goal: I want throughput without certifying teachers through a biased black box we cannot explain to applicants.

agents.name: David Okafor
agents.identity: I am the provost desperate for physics teachers before Labor Day and willing to accept slower panels only if budgets add reviewers overnight.
agents.goal: I want hybrid AI plus human review with published appeal pathways because empty classrooms harm kids more than imperfect scoring.

choices: I will suspend AI scoring and surge paid human panels with overtime cleared by the legislature's emergency line item, accepting unfilled classes if panels cannot finish in time.
I will keep AI scoring but mandate blinded human rescoring for any applicant flagged by accent-proxy features, accepting higher costs and possible union grievances on workload.
forced_choice.call_to_action: School boards are suing each other for teacher poaching and need Dr. Catherine Blackwell's policy faxed tonight. Dr. Catherine Blackwell must choose between suspending AI with surged panels or AI with mandatory human rescoring. What does Dr. Catherine Blackwell do?
